# Azure Data Lake Storage Connector - Full Documentation

# Azure Data Lake Storage Connector Examples

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The following Azure Data Lake Storage Connector examples show you how to:

- [Create an Azure Data Lake Storage file system](#create-file-system-example).
- [Create a path inside an Azure Data Lake Storage file system](#create-path-example).
- [Update a path inside an Azure Data Lake Storage file system](#update-path-example).
- [Implement error handling](#error-handling-example).

These examples use variables for some field values. You can either:

- Replace the variables with their values in the code.
- Provide the values for each variable in a properties file and then refer to that file from the
  connector configuration.

For information about the benefits of using property placeholders and how to configure them, see
[Anypoint Connector Configuration](../../connectors/introduction/intro-connector-configuration-overview).

## Create an Azure Data Lake Storage file system

This example shows you how to create a file system in Azure Data Lake Storage, and includes these
steps:

1.  [Set up the app flow](#set-up-flow).
2.  [Configure the File System operation](#configure-file-system-operation).
3.  [Optionally, copy and paste the XML](#xml-creating-file-system) for the flow in to the
    **Configuration XML** tab.

### Set Up the App Flow

1.  Create a new Mule project in Studio.
2.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `http` and select the **Listener** operation.
3.  Drag the **Listener** operation onto the Studio canvas.
4.  In the **Listener** configuration, click **+** next to the **Connector configuration** field to
    add a global element.
5.  Accept the defaults and click **OK**.

    ![HTTP Listener with default values](_images/azure-data-lake-create-file-system-setting-up-the-flow-1.png)

6.  In the **General** properties tab, set the **Path** field to `create-fs`:

![HTTP Listener General properties tab with Path value set to create fs](_images/azure-data-lake-create-file-system-setting-up-the-flow-2.png)

### Configure the Create File System Operation

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `azure` and select the **Azure Data Lake Storage
    Connector > Create File System** operation.
2.  Drag the **Create File System** operation from the **Mule Palette** onto the **Listener** flow.
3.  In the configuration screen for the operation, click the plus sign (**+**) next to the
    **Connector configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.
4.  On the **General** tab, in **Connection**, select the authentication method to configure and
    enter all the required values:

    ![Create File System Config with Shared Access Signature connection values](_images/azure-data-lake-create-file-system-filling-values-1.png)

5.  Click **OK**.
6.  On the **General** properties tab, in the **File System** field, enter a name for the file
    system:

    ![Create File System operation General properties tab](_images/azure-data-lake-create-file-system-filling-values-2.png)

7.  Save the project.
8.  Open a browser and send a GET request to `localhost:8081/create-fs` to test your app.

### XML for Creating a File System

Paste this code into the **Configuration XML** tab of your flow to quickly load the flow for this
example into your Mule app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
xmlns:azure-data-lake-storage="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-data-lake-storage"
xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
xsi:schemaLocation="
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-data-lake-storage http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-data-lake-storage/current/mule-azure-data-lake-storage.xsd">
<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config">
<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
</http:listener-config>
<azure-data-lake-storage:config name="Azure_Data_Lake_Storage_Connector_Config" doc:name="Azure Data Lake Storage Connector Config">
<azure-data-lake-storage:shared-access-signature-connection accountName="${account.name}" dnsSuffix="${dns.suffix}" sasToken="${sas.token}" />
</azure-data-lake-storage:config>
<flow name="create-file-system-flow">
<http:listener doc:name="Listener" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="create-fs"/>
<azure-data-lake-storage:create-file-system doc:name="Create File System" config-ref="Azure_Data_Lake_Storage_Connector_Config" filesystem="newfilesystem"/>
</flow>

</mule>
```

## Create a Path

This example shows you how to create a path in Azure Data Lake Storage and includes the following
steps:

1.  [Set up the app flow](#set-up-create-path-flow).
2.  [Configure the Create Path operation](#configure-create-path).
3.  [Optionally, copy and paste the XML](#create-path-xml) for the flow in to the **Configuration
    XML** tab.

### Set Up the App Flow

Follow these steps to create and configure an HTTP **Listener** source to initiate the flow each
time it receives a request on the configured host and port:

1.  Create a new Mule project in Studio.
2.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `http` and select the **Listener** operation:
3.  Drag the **Listener** operation onto the Studio canvas.
4.  In the **Listener** configuration, click **+** next to the **Connector configuration** field to
    add a global element.
5.  Accept the defaults and click **OK**.
6.  In the **General** properties tab, set the **Path** field to `create-path`:

![HTTP Listener General properties tab](_images/azure-data-lake-create-path-setting-up-the-flow-2.png)

### Configure the Create Path operation

Follow these steps to create a file or directory on the provided Azure Data Lake Storage file
system:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `azure` and select the **Create/Rename Path**
    operation.
2.  Drag the **Create/Rename Path** operation from the Studio canvas onto the **Listener** flow.
3.  In the **Create/Rename Path** configuration, click **+** next to the **Connector Configuration**
    field to add a connection configuration.
4.  On the **General** tab, in **Connection**, select the authentication method to configure and
    enter all the required values:

    ![Create File System Config with Shared Access Signature connection values](_images/azure-data-lake-create-file-system-filling-values-1.png)

5.  Click **OK**.
6.  In the **File System** field, enter the value for your target Azure Data Lake Storage file
    system.  
    If the file system does not exist, an error is thrown.
7.  In **Path**, enter your desired path name.
8.  In **Resource**, enter either `file` or `directory`:

    ![General properties configuration for Create/Rename Path operation](_images/azure-data-lake-create-path-filling-values.png)

9.  Save the project.
10. Test the app by sending a GET request to `localhost:8081/create-path`.

### XML for Creating a Path

Paste this code into the **Configuration XML** tab of your flow to quickly load the flow for this
example into your Mule app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns:azure-data-lake-storage="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-data-lake-storage"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="
		http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
		http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
		http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-data-lake-storage http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-data-lake-storage/current/mule-azure-data-lake-storage.xsd">
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config">
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<azure-data-lake-storage:config name="Azure_Data_Lake_Storage_Connector_Config" doc:name="Azure Data Lake Storage Connector Config">
		<azure-data-lake-storage:shared-access-signature-connection accountName="${account.name}" dnsSuffix="${dns.suffix}" sasToken="${sas.token}" />
	</azure-data-lake-storage:config>
	<flow name="create-path-flow">
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="create-path"/>
		<azure-data-lake-storage:create-or-rename doc:name="Create/Rename Path" config-ref="Azure_Data_Lake_Storage_Connector_Config" fileSystem="newfilesystem" path="newpath" resource="directory"/>
	</flow>

</mule>
```

## Configure Update Path Operations with Append and Flush

In this example, you configure two **Update Path** operations:

- The first **Update Path** operation is configured with **append** to append contents to an
  existing file on the Azure Data Lake Storage file system.  
  To do this, you first set up the app flow, add the File Connector **Read** operation, configure
  the **Set Variable** component, then configure the **Update Path** operation with **append**.
- The second **Update Path** operation is then configured with **flush** to flush the contents of an
  existing file on the Azure Data Lake Storage file system.

### Set Up the App Flow

Follow these steps to create and configure an HTTP **Listener** source to initiate the flow each
time it receives a request on the configured host and port:

1.  Create a new Mule project in Studio.
2.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `http` and select the **Listener** operation:
3.  Drag the **Listener** operation onto the Studio canvas.
4.  In the **Listener** configuration, click **+** next to the **Connector configuration** field to
    add a global element.
5.  Accept the defaults and click **OK**.
6.  In the **General** properties tab, set the **Path** field to `update-path`:

![Listener General properties configuration](_images/azure-data-lake-update-path-setting-up-the-flow-2.png)

### Add and Configure File Connector

Add File Connector to your project and configure the **Read** operation to read files on the Azure
Data Lake Storage file system:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
2.  In **Add Modules to Project**, type `file connector` in the search field.
3.  Click **File Connector** in **Available modules**.
4.  Click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.

    ![File Connector added from Exchange to your flow](_images/azure-data-lake-update-path-adding-file-connector.png)

6.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **File > Read** and drag the **Read** operation onto the
    flow.
7.  In **General** properties tab for the **Read** operation, click **+** next to the **Connector
    Configuration** field to add a connection configuration.
8.  Enter the values and click **OK**.
9.  In the **General** properties for the **Read** operation, enter the absolute path to the file
    upload in **File Path**:

    ![Read operation General properties configuration](_images/azure-data-lake-update-path-using-the-file-connector-2.png)

### Configure the Set Variable Component

Configure a **Set Variable** component to store and use a variable later in the flow:

1.  From the **Mule Palette** view, search for `set variable` and drag **Set Variable** next to the
    **Read** operation on the Studio canvas.
2.  In the **General** properties tab, enter these values:
    - **Name**  
      `fileSize`
    - **Value**  
      `#[attributes.size]`\+ This saves the length of the file variable for the next operations.

![General properties configuration for the Set Variable component](_images/azure-data-lake-update-path-using-the-file-connector-3.png)

### Configure the Update Path Operation with Append

Configure the **Update Path** operation to add **append** so you can append contents to a file:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `azure` and select the Azure Data Lake Storage
    Connector **Update Path** operation.
2.  Drag the **Update Path** operation from the **Mule Palette** view onto the **Listener** flow,
    next to the **Set Variable** component.
3.  In the **General** configuration properties pane of **Update Path**, select the connection
    configuration for the operation.
4.  In the **General** properties tab, enter the following values:
    - **File System**  
      Value for your target Azure Data Lake Storage file system
    - **Path**  
      Value for your target path
    - **Action**  
      `append`
    - **Position**  
      `0`
    - **Content Length**  
      `#[vars.fileSize]`
    - **Content**  
      `payload`

      ![General configuration properties with example values for the Update Path operation with append](_images/azure-data-lake-update-path-using-update-append.png)

5.  Save the project.

### Configure the Update Path Operation with Flush

Configure the **Update Path** operation to add **flush** so you can flush the contents of a file:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `azure` and select the Azure Data Lake Storage
    Connector **Update Path** operation.
2.  Drag the **Update Path** operation from the Studio canvas onto the **Listener** flow, next to
    the **Update Path** operation that you created in [the step for append](#update-path-append).
3.  In the **General** configuration properties pane of **Update Path**, select the connection
    configuration for the operation.
4.  In the **General** properties tab, enter the following values:
    - **File System**  
      Your target Azure Data Lake Storage file system
    - **Path**  
      Your target path
    - **Action**  
      `flush`
    - **Position**  
      `#[vars.fileSize]`
    - **Content Length**  
      `0`

      ![General configuration properties with example values for the Update Path operation with flush](_images/azure-data-lake-update-path-using-update-flush.png)

5.  Save the project.
6.  Open a browser and send a GET request to `localhost:8081/update-path` to test the app.

### XML for Updating a Path

Paste this code into the **Configuration XML** tab of your flow to quickly load the flow for this
example into your Mule app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:file="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns:azure-data-lake-storage="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-data-lake-storage"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="
		http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
		http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
		http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-data-lake-storage http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-data-lake-storage/current/mule-azure-data-lake-storage.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd">
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config">
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<azure-data-lake-storage:config name="Azure_Data_Lake_Storage_Connector_Config" doc:name="Azure Data Lake Storage Connector Config">
		<azure-data-lake-storage:shared-access-signature-connection accountName="${account.name}" dnsSuffix="${dns.suffix}" sasToken="${sas.token}" />
	</azure-data-lake-storage:config>
	<file:config name="File_Config" doc:name="File Config"/>
	<flow name="create-path-flow">
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="update-path"/>
		<file:read doc:name="Read" config-ref="File_Config" path="${file.path}"/>
		<set-variable value="#[attributes.size]" doc:name="Set Variable" variableName="fileSize"/>
		<azure-data-lake-storage:update-path doc:name="Update Path" config-ref="Azure_Data_Lake_Storage_Connector_Config" fileSystem="${filesystem.name}" path="${path.name}" action="append" position="0" contentLength="#[vars.fileSize]"/>
		<azure-data-lake-storage:update-path doc:name="Update Path" config-ref="Azure_Data_Lake_Storage_Connector_Config" fileSystem="${filesystem.name}" path="${path.name}" action="flush" position="#[vars.fileSize]" contentLength="0"/>
	</flow>

</mule>
```

## Implement Error Handling

This section shows you how to implement simple error handling on a flow that uses Azure Data Lake
Storage Connector.

1.  In any flow, from the **Mule Palette** view, select **Core > On Error Continue** and drag it to
    the **Error Handling** section of the flow:

    ![On Error Continue component dragged to the flow](_images/azure-data-lake-error-handling-setting-up-1.png)

2.  From the **Mule Palette** view, drag **Core > Transform Message** to the flow:

    ![Transform Message component dragged to the flow](_images/azure-data-lake-error-handling-setting-up-2.png)

3.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `logger` and drag the **Logger** component to the right
    of **Transform Message**.
4.  In the **General** properties tab for the **Logger** component, enter the following values:

    output application/json

    ***

    error.errorMessage.payload

    To access the error code that comes from the Azure service, the expression is:  
    `error.muleMessage.typedValue.error.code`

    ![Expression to access the error code that comes from the Azure service](_images/azure-data-lake-error-handling-setting-up-3.png)

    To access the error message, the expression is:  
    `error.muleMessage.typedValue.error.message`

![Expression to access the error message](_images/azure-data-lake-error-handling-setting-up-4.png)

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)

# Azure Data Lake Storage Connector Module Connector Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

## Configurations

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Config_Oauth20">OAuth 2.0 Connection Provider</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#Config_SharedAccessSignature">Shared Access Signature</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#Config_SharedKey">Shared Key Connection</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The connection types that can be provided to this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td><p>MINUTES</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to <code>AUTO</code> will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td><p>AUTO</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The identifier of this element used in other components to reference it</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration. This does not mean that the instance expires at the exact moment that it becomes eligible. Mule purges the instances as appropriate.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Connection Types

### OAuth 2.0 Connection Provider

Use OAuth 2.0 to delegate user authentication to the service hosting the user account. For more
information, refer to
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2) or
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors on CloudHub](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2-cloudhub).

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Default Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Default Header</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultQueryParam">Default Query Param</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for establishing connections to the remote service</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Connection Timeout</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Persistent Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If false, each connection is closed after the first request is completed.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of outbound connections that to keep open at the same time</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A timeout for how long a connection can remain idle before it is closed</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the connection Idle Timeout</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Proxy Config</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Reusable configuration element for outbound connections through a proxy</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Stream Response</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether or not received responses should be streamed</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The space in bytes for the buffer where the HTTP response will be stored.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Base URI</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Parameter base URI, each instance/tenant gets its own</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies the version of the REST API protocol used for processing the request</p></td><td><p>2018-11-09</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment will fail if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Shared Access Signature

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Default Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Default Header</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultQueryParam">Default Query Param</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for establishing connections to the remote service</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Connection Timeout</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Persistent Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If false, each connection will be closed after the first request is completed.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of outbound connections that will be kept open at the same time</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A timeout for how long a connection can remain idle before it is closed</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the connection Idle Timeout</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Proxy Config</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Reusable configuration element for outbound connections through a proxy</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Stream Response</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether or not to stream received responses</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The space in bytes for the buffer where the HTTP response is stored.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Storage account name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Storage account name</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>DNS Suffix</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>DNS suffix of the storage. The DNS suffix works only with <code>dfs.core.windows.net</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Sas Token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>SAS token for the Azure Data Lake Storage account.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Shared Key Connection

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Default Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Default Header</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultQueryParam">Default Query Param</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for establishing connections to the remote service</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Connection Timeout</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Persistent Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If false, each connection is closed after the first request is completed.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of outbound connections to keep open concurrently</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A timeout for how long a connection can remain idle before it is closed</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the connection Idle Timeout</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Proxy Config</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Reusable configuration element for outbound connections through a proxy</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Stream Response</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether or not to stream received responses</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The space in bytes for the buffer where the HTTP response is stored.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Storage account name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Storage account name</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>DNS Suffix</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>DNS suffix of the storage. The DNS suffix works only with <code>dfs.core.windows.net</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Version</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies the version of the REST protocol used for processing the request</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Account Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Password used to access the Azure Data Lake Storage account.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## List of Supported Operations

- [Create File System](#CreateFileSystem)
- [Create/Rename Path](#CreateOrRename)
- [Delete File System](#DeleteFileSystem)
- [Delete Path](#DeletePath)
- [Get Path Properties](#GetPathProperties)
- [Lease path](#LeasePath)
- [List File Systems](#ListFileSystems)
- [List Paths](#ListPaths)
- [Read Path](#ReadPath)
- [Update Path](#UpdatePath)

## Create File System

`<azure-data-lake-storage:create-file-system>`

Creates a file system on the storage account.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File System</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Target file system.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional. Timeout value expressed in seconds.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Properties</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>User-defined properties to store with the file or directory, in the format of a comma-separated list of name and value pairs, for example, <code>n1=v1, n2=v2, â¦â</code>, where each value is a Base64-encoded string.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Client Request ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A UUID recorded in the analytics logs for troubleshooting and correlation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to <code>AUTO</code> will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:BAD_REQUEST
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:CLIENT_ERROR
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:TIMEOUT
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:UNAUTHORIZED
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Create/Rename Path

`<azure-data-lake-storage:create-or-rename>`

Creates or renames a file or directory path on the provided file system.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File System</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Target file system.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Target path.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Resource</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only required for creation. The value must be <code>file</code> or <code>directory</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Continuation</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional. When renaming a directory, the number of paths that are renamed with each invocation is limited.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Mode</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional. Valid only when namespace is enabled. This parameter determines the behavior of the rename operation. The value must be <code>legacy</code> or <code>posix</code>, and the default value is <code>posix</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Rename Source</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An optional file or directory to rename. The value must have the following format:`/{filesystem}/{path}`.</p></div><div><p>If <code>x-ms-properties</code> is specified it overwrites the existing properties; otherwise, the existing properties are preserved. This value must be a URL percent-encoded string and the string can contain only ASCII characters in the ISO-8859-1 format.</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional. Timeout value expressed in seconds.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Client Request ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A UUID recorded in the analytics logs for troubleshooting and correlation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Cache Control</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The service stores this value and includes it in the "Cache-Control" response header for <code>Read Path</code> operations.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which content encodings are applied to the file. This value is returned to the client when the <code>Read Path</code> operation is performed.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content Language</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional. Specifies the natural language used by the intended audience for the file.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content Disposition</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The service stores this value and includes it in the "Content-Disposition" response header for <code>Read Path</code> operations.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Cache Control</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The service stores this value and includes it in the "Cache-Control" response header for <code>Read Path</code> operations.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Content Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The service stores this value and includes it in the "Content-Type" response header for <code>Read Path</code> operations.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Content Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The service stores this value and includes it in the "Content-Encoding" response header for <code>Read Path</code> operations.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Content Language</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The service stores this value and includes it in the "Content-Language" response header for <code>Read Path</code> operations.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Content Disposition</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The service stores this value and includes it in the "Content-Disposition" response header for <code>Read Path</code> operations.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Lease ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A lease ID for the path specified in the URI. The path to overwrite must have an active lease and the lease ID must match.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Source Lease ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional for rename operations. A lease ID for the source path. The source path must have an active lease and the lease ID must match.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Properties</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>User-defined properties to store with the file or directory, in the format of a comma-separated list of name and value pairs, for example, <code>n1=v1, n2=v2, â¦â</code>, where each value is a Base64-encoded string.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Permissions</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional and only valid if <strong>Hierarchical Namespace</strong> is enabled for the account. Sets POSIX access permissions for the file owner, the file owning group, and others.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Umask</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional and only valid if <strong>Hierarchical Namespace</strong> is enabled for the account. When creating a file or directory, if the parent folder does not have a default ACL, the umask restricts the permissions of the file or directory to be created.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If Match</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An ETag value. Specify this header to perform the operation only if the resourceâs ETag matches the value specified. The ETag must be specified in quotes.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If None Match</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An ETag value or the special wildcard (<code>*</code>) value. Specify this header to perform the operation only if the resourceâs ETag does not match the value specified. The ETag must be specified in quotes.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If Modified Since</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A date and time value. Specify this header to perform the operation only if the resource has been modified since the specified date and time.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If Unmodified Since</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A date and time value. Specify this header to perform the operation only if the resource has not been modified since the specified date and time.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Source If Match</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An ETag value. Specify this header to perform the rename operation only if the sourceâs ETag matches the value specified. The ETag must be specified in quotes.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Source If None Match</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An ETag value or the special wildcard (<code>*</code>) value. Specify this header to perform the rename operation only if the sourceâs ETag does not match the value specified. The ETag must be specified in quotes.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Source If Modified Since</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A date and time value. Specify this header to perform the rename operation only if the source has been modified since the specified date and time.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Source If Unmodified Since</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A date and time value. Specify this header to perform the rename operation only if the source has not been modified since the specified date and time.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:BAD_REQUEST
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:CLIENT_ERROR
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:TIMEOUT
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:UNAUTHORIZED
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Delete File System

`<azure-data-lake-storage:delete-file-system>`

Deletes a file system on the Storage account.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File System</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Target file system.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional. Timeout value expressed in seconds.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If Modified Since</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional. A date and time value. Specify this header to perform the operation only if the resource has been modified since the specified date and time.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If Unmodified Since</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional. A date and time value. Specify this header to perform the operation only if the resource has not been modified since the specified date and time.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Properties</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>User-defined properties to store with the file or directory, in the format of a comma-separated list of name and value pairs, for example, <code>n1=v1, n2=v2, â¦â</code>, where each value is a Base64-encoded string.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Client Request ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A UUID recorded in the analytics logs for troubleshooting and correlation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:BAD_REQUEST
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:CLIENT_ERROR
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:TIMEOUT
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:UNAUTHORIZED
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Delete Path

`<azure-data-lake-storage:delete-path>`

Deletes a path.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File System</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Target file system.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Target path.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Continuation</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When deleting a directory, the number of paths that are deleted with each invocation is limited.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Recursive</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Required for directory only. If <code>true</code>, all paths beneath the directory are deleted. If <code>false</code> and the directory is non-empty, an error occurs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional. Timeout value expressed in seconds.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Client Request ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A UUID recorded in the analytics logs for troubleshooting and correlation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Lease ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A lease ID for the path specified in the URI. The path to overwrite must have an active lease and the lease ID must match.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If Match</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An ETag value. Specify this header to perform the operation only if the resourceâs ETag matches the value specified. The ETag must be specified in quotes.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If None Match</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An ETag value or the special wildcard ("*") value. Specify this header to perform the operation only if the resourceâs ETag does not match the value specified. The ETag must be specified in quotes.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If Modified Since</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A date and time value. Specify this header to perform the operation only if the resource has been modified since the specified date and time.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If Unmodified Since</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A date and time value. Specify this header to perform the operation only if the resource has not been modified since the specified date and time.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:BAD_REQUEST
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:CLIENT_ERROR
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:TIMEOUT
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:UNAUTHORIZED
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Get Path Properties

`<azure-data-lake-storage:get-path-properties>`

Retrieves the properties of a path.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File System</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Target file system.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Target path.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Action</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional. If the value is <code>getStatus</code>, only the system-defined properties for the path are returned. If the value is <code>getAccessControl</code>, the access control list is returned in the response headers if <strong>Hierarchical Namespace</strong> is enabled for the account, otherwise, the properties are returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional. Timeout value expressed in seconds.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>UPN</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional. Valid only when <strong>Hierarchical Namespace</strong> is enabled for the account. If <code>true</code>, the user identity values returned in the Owner and Group fields of each list entry are transformed from Azure Active Directory Object IDs to User Principal Names. If <code>false</code>, the values are returned as Azure Active Directory Object IDs. The default value is <code>false</code>. Group and application Object IDs are not translated because they do not have unique friendly names.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Lease ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A lease ID for the path specified in the URI. The path to overwrite must have an active lease and the lease ID must match.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If Match</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An ETag value. Specify this header to perform the operation only if the resourceâs ETag matches the value specified. The ETag must be specified in quotes.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If None Match</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An ETag value or the special wildcard ("*") value. Specify this header to perform the operation only if the resourceâs ETag does not match the value specified. The ETag must be specified in quotes.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If Modified Since</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional. A date and time value. Specify this header to perform the operation only if the resource has been modified since the specified date and time.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If Unmodified Since</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional. A date and time value. Specify this header to perform the operation only if the resource has not been modified since the specified date and time.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Client Request ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A UUID recorded in the analytics logs for troubleshooting and correlation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to be used to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:BAD_REQUEST
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:CLIENT_ERROR
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:TIMEOUT
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:UNAUTHORIZED
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Lease path

`<azure-data-lake-storage:lease-path>`

Create and manage a lease to restrict write and delete access to the path.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File System</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Target file system.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Target path.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Lease Action</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The lease action, which can be: <code>acquire</code>, <code>break</code>, <code>change</code>, <code>renew</code>, and <code>release</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Lease ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Required when "x-ms-lease-action" is <code>renew</code>, <code>change</code>, or <code>release</code>. For the <code>renew</code> and <code>release</code> actions, this must match the current lease ID.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Lease Duration</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The lease duration is required to acquire a lease, and specifies the duration of the lease in seconds. The lease duration must be between 15 and 60 seconds or -1 for an infinite lease.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Proposed Lease ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Required when "X-MS-Lease Action" is <code>acquire</code> or <code>change</code>. A lease will be acquired with this lease ID if the operation is successful.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional. Timeout value expressed in seconds.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Lease Break Period</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional. Breaks a lease, and specifies the break period duration of the lease in seconds. The lease break duration must be between 0 and 60 seconds.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If Match</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional. An ETag value. Specify this header to perform the operation only if the resourceâs ETag matches the value specified. The ETag must be specified in quotes.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If None Match</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional. An ETag value or the special wildcard ("*") value. Specify this header to perform the operation only if the resourceâs ETag does not match the value specified. The ETag must be specified in quotes.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If Modified Since</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional. A date and time value. Specify this header to perform the operation only if the resource has been modified since the specified date and time.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If Unmodified Since</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional. A date and time value. Specify this header to perform the operation only if the resource has not been modified since the specified date and time.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Client Request ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A UUID recorded in the analytics logs for troubleshooting and correlation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In-Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#non-repeatable-stream">Non-Repeatable Stream</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:BAD_REQUEST
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:CLIENT_ERROR
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:TIMEOUT
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:UNAUTHORIZED
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## List File Systems

`<azure-data-lake-storage:list-file-systems>`

Lists all the file systems on the account.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Continuation</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of file systems returned with each invocation is limited. If the number of file systems to return exceeds this limit, a continuation token is returned in the response header x-ms-continuation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Results</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>An optional value that specifies the maximum number of items to return. If omitted, or greater than 5,000, the response will include up to 5,000 items.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Prefix</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Filters results to file systems within the specified prefix.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional. Timeout value expressed in seconds.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Client Request ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A UUID recorded in the analytics logs for troubleshooting and correlation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In-Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#non-repeatable-stream">Non-Repeatable Stream</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>
