- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:BAD_REQUEST
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:CLIENT_ERROR
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:TIMEOUT
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:UNAUTHORIZED
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## List Paths

`<azure-data-lake-storage:list-paths>`

List all paths of a file system.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File System</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Target file system.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Recursive</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If <code>true</code>, all paths are listed; otherwise, only paths at the root of the file system are listed. If <code>directory</code> is specified, the list only includes paths that share the same root.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Continuation</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The number of paths returned with each invocation is limited. If the number of paths to return exceeds this limit, a continuation token is returned in the response header x-ms-continuation. When a continuation token is returned in the response, it must be specified in a subsequent invocation of the list operation to continue listing the paths.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Directory</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Filters results to paths within the specified directory. An error occurs if the directory does not exist.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Results</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>An optional value that specifies the maximum number of items to return. If omitted, or greater than 5,000, the response will include up to 5,000 items.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional. Timeout value expressed in seconds.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>UPN</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional. Valid only when <strong>Hierarchical Namespace</strong> is enabled for the account. If <code>true</code>, the user identity values returned in the owner and group fields of each list entry will be transformed from Azure Active Directory Object IDs to User Principal Names. If <code>false</code>, the values are returned as Azure Active Directory Object IDs. The default value is <code>false</code>. Group and application Object IDs are not translated because they do not have unique friendly names.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Client Request ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A UUID recorded in the analytics logs for troubleshooting and correlation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to be used to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In-Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#non-repeatable-stream">Non-Repeatable Stream</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout}</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:BAD_REQUEST
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:CLIENT_ERROR
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:TIMEOUT
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:UNAUTHORIZED
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Read Path

`<azure-data-lake-storage:read-path>`

Read the contents of a file.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File System</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Target file system.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Target path.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Range</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The HTTP range request header specifies one or more byte ranges of the resource to be retrieved.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional. Timeout value expressed in seconds.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Lease ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A lease ID for the path specified in the URI. The path to overwrite must have an active lease and the lease ID must match.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Range Get Content MD5</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional. When this header is set to <code>true</code> and specified together with the Range header, the service returns the MD5 hash for the range, as long as the range is less than or equal to 4MB in size.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If Match</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An ETag value. Specify this header to perform the operation only if the resourceâs ETag matches the value specified. The ETag must be specified in quotes.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If None Match</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An ETag value or the special wildcard ("*") value. Specify this header to perform the operation only if the resourceâs ETag does not match the value specified. The ETag must be specified in quotes.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If Modified Since</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A date and time value. Specify this header to perform the operation only if the resource has been modified since the specified date and time.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If Unmodified Since</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A date and time value. Specify this header to perform the operation only if the resource has not been modified since the specified date and time.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Client Request ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A UUID recorded in the analytics logs for troubleshooting and correlation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In-Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#non-repeatable-stream">Non-Repeatable Stream</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:BAD_REQUEST
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:CLIENT_ERROR
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:TIMEOUT
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:UNAUTHORIZED
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Update Path

`<azure-data-lake-storage:update-path>`

Uploads data to append to a file, flushes (writes) previously uploaded data to a file, sets
properties for a file or directory, or sets access control for a file or directory.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File System</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Target file system.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Target path.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Action</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Action to perform, must be <code>append</code> or <code>flush</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Position</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Allows the caller to upload data in parallel and control the order in which it is appended to the file.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content Length</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Required for Append or Flush data. Must be <code>0</code> for Flush. Must be the length of the request content in bytes for Append.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><p>The payload to upload.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional. Timeout value expressed in seconds.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Close</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Allow applications to receive notifications when files change.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Retain Uncommitted Data</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Valid for flush operations. If true, uncommitted data is retained after the flush operation completes. Defaults to false.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content MD5</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An MD5 hash of the request content. This header is valid on <code>Append</code> and <code>Flush</code> operations.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Lease ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A lease ID for the path specified in the URI. The path to overwrite must have an active lease and the lease ID must match.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Cache Control</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The service stores this value and includes it in the "Cache-Control" response header for <code>Read Path</code> operations.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Content Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The service stores this value and includes it in the "Content-Type" response header for <code>Read Path</code> operations.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Content Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The service stores this value and includes it in the "Content-Encoding" response header for <code>Read Path</code> operations.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Content Language</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The service stores this value and includes it in the "Content-Language" response header for <code>Read Path</code> operations.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Content Disposition</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The service stores this value and includes it in the "Content-Disposition" response header for <code>Read Path</code> operations.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Content MD5</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional and only valid for <strong>Flush &amp; Set Properties</strong> operations. The service stores this value and includes it in the "Content-Md5" response header for <strong>Read &amp; Get Properties</strong> operations.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Properties</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>User-defined properties to store with the file or directory, in the format of a comma-separated list of name and value pairs, for example, <code>n1=v1, n2=v2, â¦â</code>, where each value is a Base64-encoded string.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Permissions</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional and only valid if <strong>Hierarchical Namespace</strong> is enabled for the account. Sets POSIX access permissions for the file owner, the file owning group, and others.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Owner</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional and valid only for the setAccessControl Operation. Sets the owner of the file or directory.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Group</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional and valid only for the setAccessControl operation. Sets the owner of the file or directory.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-ACL</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional and valid only for the setAccessControl operation. Sets POSIX access control rights on files and directories.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If Match</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An ETag value. Specify this header to perform the operation only if the resourceâs ETag matches the value specified. The ETag must be specified in quotes.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If None Match</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An ETag value or the special wildcard ("*") value. Specify this header to perform the operation only if the resourceâs ETag does not match the value specified. The ETag must be specified in quotes.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If Modified Since</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A date and time value. Specify this header to perform the operation only if the resource has been modified since the specified date and time.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If Unmodified Since</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A date and time value. Specify this header to perform the operation only if the resource has not been modified since the specified date and time.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>X-MS-Client Request ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A UUID recorded in the analytics logs for troubleshooting and correlation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to be used to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In-Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#non-repeatable-stream">Non-Repeatable Stream</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configure how Mule processes streams with streaming strategies. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines if the request should be sent using streaming. Setting the value to AUTO will automatically define the best strategy based on the request content.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the <strong>Target variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#configurations)

### Throws

- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:BAD_REQUEST
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:CLIENT_ERROR
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:SERVER_ERROR
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:TIMEOUT
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:UNAUTHORIZED
- AZURE-DATA-LAKE-STORAGE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Types

### Default Header

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Default Query Param

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Proxy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Host</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Port</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Non Proxy Hosts</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### TLS

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Enabled Protocols</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of protocols enabled for this context.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enabled Cipher Suites</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of cipher suites enabled for this context.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Trust Store</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TrustStore">Trust Store</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key Store</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KeyStore">Key Store</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Revocation Check</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#StandardRevocationCheck">Standard Revocation Check</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CustomOcspResponder">Custom OCSP Responder</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CrlFile">CRL File</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Trust Store

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The location (which will be resolved relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible) of the truststore.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The password used to protect the truststore.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The type of truststore used.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The algorithm used by the truststore.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Insecure</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If true, no certificate validations will be performed, rendering connections vulnerable to attacks. Use at your own risk.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Key Store

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The location (which will be resolved relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible) of the keystore.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The type of keystore used.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Alias</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When the keystore contains many private keys, this attribute indicates the alias of the key to use. If not defined, the first key in the file is used by default.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The password used to protect the private key.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The password used to protect the keystore.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The algorithm used by the keystore.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Standard Revocation Check

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Only End Entities</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only verify the last element of the certificate chain.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Prefer Crls</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Try CRL instead of OCSP first.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>No Fallback</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Do not use the secondary checking method (the one not selected before).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Soft Fail</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Avoid verification failure when the revocation server can not be reached or is busy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Custom OCSP Responder

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The URL of the OCSP responder.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Cert Alias</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Alias of the signing certificate for the OCSP response (must be in the trust store), if present.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### CRL File

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The path to the CRL file.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Fails Deployment</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment will fail if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The reconnection strategy to use</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Frequency</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How often (in ms) to reconnect</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Blocking</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If false, the reconnection strategy will run in a separate, non-blocking thread</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Count</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How many reconnection attempts to make</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Frequency</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How often (in ms) to reconnect</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Blocking</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If false, the reconnection strategy will run in a separate, non-blocking thread</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Max Idle Time</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A scalar time value for the maximum amount of time a dynamic configuration instance should be allowed to be idle before itâs considered eligible for expiration</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the maxIdleTime attribute</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### HTTP Response Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Status Code</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reason Phrase</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In-Memory Stream

When streaming in this mode, Mule does not use the disk to buffer the contents. If you exceed the
buffer size, the message fails.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Initial Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This is the amount of memory to allocate to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream contains more data than what can fit into this buffer, then it expands according to the bufferSizeIncrement attribute, with an upper limit of maxInMemorySize.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Buffer Size Increment</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This specifies the increment for how much the buffer size will expand if it exceeds its initial specified size. Setting a value of zero or lower means that the buffer should not expand, and a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error is raised when the buffer gets full.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This is the maximum amount of memory to use. If more than that is used then a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error is raised. A value lower or equal to zero means no limit.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Buffer Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The unit in which all these attributes are expressed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Stream

File store repeatable streams require buffering, and there are different buffering strategies. Mule
keeps a portion of contents in memory. If the stream contents are larger than the configured buffer
size, Mule backs up the bufferâs content to disk and then clears the memory.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In Memory Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the maximum memory that the stream should use to keep data in memory. If more than that is consumed then it will start to buffer the content on disk.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Buffer Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The unit in which maxInMemorySize is expressed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Non-Repeatable Stream

Disables the repeatable stream functionality and use non-repeatable streams to have less performance
overhead, memory use, and cost.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Using Anypoint Studio to Configure Azure Data Lake Storage Connector

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Studio (Studio) editors help you design and update your Mule applications, properties, and
configuration files.

To add and configure a connector in Studio:

1.  [Create a Mule project](#create-mule-project).
2.  [Add the connector to your Mule project](#add-connector-to-project).
3.  [Configure an input source for the connectorâs flow](#configure-input-source).
4.  [Add a connector operation to the flow](#add-connector-operation).
5.  [Configure a global element for the connector](#configure-global-element).

When you run the connector, you can view the app log to check for problems, as described in
[View the App Log](#view-app-log).

If you are new to configuring connectors in Studio, see
[Using Anypoint Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio).
If, after reading this topic, you need additional information about the connector fields, see the
[Azure Data Lake Storage Connector Reference](azure-data-lake-connector-reference).

## Create a Mule Project

In Studio, create a new Mule project in which to add and configure the connector:

1.  In Studio, select **File > New > Mule Project**.
2.  Enter a name for your Mule project and click **Finish**.

## Add the Connector to Your Mule Project

Add Azure Data Lake Storage Connector to your Mule project to automatically populate the XML code
with the connectorâs namespace and schema location and add the required dependencies to the
projectâs `pom.xml` file:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
2.  In **Add Modules to Project**, type `azure data lake storage` in the search field.
3.  Click Azure Data Lake Storage in **Available modules**.
4.  Click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.

Adding a connector to a Mule project in Studio does not make that connector available to other
projects in your Studio workspace.

## Configure a Source

A source initiates a flow when a specified condition is met. You can configure one of these sources
to use with Azure Data Lake Storage Connector:

- **HTTP > Listener**  
  Initiates a flow each time it receives a request on the configured host and port
- **Scheduler**  
  Initiates a flow when a time-based condition is met

For example, to configure an HTTP listener, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **HTTP > Listener**.
2.  Drag **Listener** to the Studio canvas.
3.  On the **Listener** configuration screen, optionally change the value of the **Display Name**
    field.
4.  Specify a value for the **Path** field.
5.  Click the plus sign (**+**) next to the **Connector configuration** field to configure a global
    element that can be used by all instances of HTTP **Listener** in the app.
6.  On the **General** tab, specify connection information for the connector.
7.  On the **TLS** tab, optionally specify the TLS information for the connector.
8.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
9.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
10. Click **OK**.

## Add a Connector Operation to the Flow

When you add a connector operation to your flow, you immediately define a specific operation for
that connector to perform.

To add an operation for Azure Data Lake Storage Connector, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **Azure Data Lake Storage** and then select the desired
    operation.
2.  Drag the operation onto the Studio canvas to the right of the input source.

## Configure a Global Element for the Connector

When you configure a connector, itâs best to configure a global element that all instances of that
connector in the app can use. Configuring a global element requires you to provide the
authentication credentials that the connector requires to access the target Azure Data Lake Storage
system. Azure Data Lake Storage Connector supports Azure Active Directory OAuth 2.0, Shared Access
Signature, and Shared Key Connection authentication methods.

To configure the global element for Azure Data Lake Storage Connector, follow these steps:

1.  Select the name of the connector in the Studio canvas.
2.  In the configuration screen for the operation, click the plus sign (**+**) next to the
    **Connector configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.
3.  On the **General** tab, in **Connection**, select the authentication method to configure:
    - **OAuth 2.0 Connection Provider** (Azure Active Directory OAuth 2.0)
    - **Shared Access Signature**
    - **Shared Key Connection**

      You can reference a configuration file that contains ANT-style property placeholders
      (recommended), or you can enter your authorization credentials in the global configuration
      properties. For information about the benefits of using property placeholders and how to
      configure them, see
      [Anypoint Connector Configuration](../../connectors/introduction/intro-connector-configuration-overview).

4.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
5.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
6.  Click **OK**.

### OAuth 2.0 Connection Provider Authentication

Use OAuth 2.0 to delegate user authentication to the service hosting the user account. For more
information, refer to
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2) or
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors on CloudHub](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2-cloudhub).

Enter the following information on the **General** tab of the **Global Element Properties** screen
to configure Azure Active Directory OAuth 2.0 authentication:

- **Base URI**  
  One per instance or tenant
- **X-MS Version**  
  Version of the REST API protocol used for processing the request
- **Client ID**  
  OAuth client ID thatâs registered with Azure
- **Client Secret**  
  Client secret that corresponds to the client ID
- **Token URL**  
  Endpoint URL of the OAuth token service provider
- **Scopes**  
  Scopes to request during the OAuth dance

The following image shows an example of the Azure Active Directory OAuth 2.0 connection
configuration values:

![Example values for the OAuth 2.0 Connection Provider connection type](_images/azure-data-lake-oauth2-connection-provider.png)

Figure 1. OAuth 2.0 Connection Provider authentication fields

### Shared Access Signature Authentication

Enter the following information on the **General** tab of the **Global Element Properties** screen
to configure **Shared Access Signature** authentication:

- **Storage Account Name**  
  Azure storage account name
- **DNS Suffix**  
  DNS suffix of the Azure storage account. In most cases itâs 'dfs.core.windows.net'.
- **SAS Token**  
  SAS token for the Azure storage account

The following image shows an example of the **Shared Access Signature** connection configuration
values:

![Example values for the Shared Access Signature connection type](_images/azure-data-lake-shared-access-config.png)

Figure 2. Shared Access Signature authentication fields

### Shared Key Connection Authentication

Enter the following information on the **General** tab of the **Global Element Properties** screen
to configure **Shared Key Connection** authentication:

- **Storage Account Name**  
  Name of the Azure Data Lake Storage account
- **DNS Suffix**  
  DNS suffix of the Azure Data Lake Storage account. In most cases itâs 'dfs.core.windows.net'.
- **X-MS Version**  
  Version of the REST API protocol used for processing the request
- **Account Key**  
  Password used to access the Azure Data Lake Storage account

## View the App Log

To check for problems, you can view the app log as follows:

- If youâre running the app from Anypoint Platform, the app log output is visible in the Anypoint
  Studio console window.
- If youâre running the app using Mule from the command line, the app log output is visible in your
  OS console.

Unless the log file path is customized in the appâs log file (`log4j2.xml`), you can also view the
app log in the default location `MULE_HOME/logs/<app-name>.log`. You can configure the location of
the log path in the app log file `log4j2.xml`.

## Next Step

After you configure a global element and connection information, you can try the
[Examples](azure-data-lake-connector-examples) for common use cases.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Azure Data Lake Storage Connector Reference](azure-data-lake-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Azure Data Lake Storage - XML and Maven Support

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Although you can manually code a Mule app in XML, it is more efficient to use Anypoint Studio.

If you manually code a Mule runtime engine (Mule) app in XML either from the Anypoint Studio XML
editor or from a text editor, you can access the connector from your app by adding reference
statements to both your XML Mule flow and the Apache Maven `pom.xml` file.

When you add the connector in Studio, Studio automatically populates the XML code with the
connectorâs namespace and schema location and it also adds a dependency to the `pom.xml` file.

## Add a Namespace for the Connector

Paste the following code inside the `<mule>` tag of the header of your configuration XML:

```xml
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-data-lake-storage
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-data-lake-storage/current/azure-data-lake-storage.xsd
```

This example shows how the namespace statements are placed in the XML:

```xml
<mule xmlns:azure-data-lake-storage="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-data-lake-storage"
	xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns:file="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-data-lake-storage
    http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-data-lake-storage/current/azure-data-lake-storage.xsd
```

## Add a POM File Dependency

The Apache Maven `pom.xml` file generated by Anypoint Studio contains dependencies for a Mule app.
When you code a Mule app manually, include this XML snippet in your `pom.xml` file to enable access
to this connector:

```xml
<dependency>
  <groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors</groupId>
  <artifactId>mule-azure-data-lake-storage-connector</artifactId>
  <version>x.x.x</version>
  <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
</dependency>
```

Replace `x.x.x` with the version that corresponds to the connector you are using.

To obtain the most up-to-date `pom.xml` file information:

1.  Go to [Anypoint Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/).
2.  In Exchange, click **Login** and supply your Anypoint Platform username and password.
3.  In Exchange, search for `azure data lake storage`.
4.  Select the connector.
5.  Click **Dependency Snippets** near the upper right of the screen.

## Next Step

After completing your namespace and `pom.xml` file, you can try the
[Examples](azure-data-lake-connector-examples).

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Azure Data Lake Storage Connector

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Azure Data Lake Storage Gen 2 (Azure Data Lake Storage Connector) provides
access to standard Azure Data Lake Storage Gen 2 operations using Anypoint Platform.

Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 is a scalable data storage service built by Microsoft Azure and
designed for big data analytics. Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 is built on top of Azure Blob Storage
and provides the data organization and security semantics of Azure Data Lake Gen1 along with the
cost and reliability benefits of Azure Blob Storage.

For information about compatibility and fixed issues, see the Azure Data Lake Storage Connector
Release Notes.

## Before You Begin

To use this connector, you must be familiar with:

- Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 API
- Anypoint Connectors
- Mule runtime engine (Mule)
- Elements and global elements in a Mule flow
- How to create a Mule app using Anypoint Studio

Before creating an app, you must have access to:

- The Azure Data Lake Storage target resource
- Anypoint Platform
- Anypoint Studio version 7.1 or later

## Common Use Cases for the Connector

Common use cases for Azure Data Lake Storage Connector include the following:

- Create, read, update, and delete files in an existing Azure Data Lake Storage file system.
- Create, rename, and delete directories from the Azure Data Lake Storage file system.
- Rename existing files.
- Append the contents of a file and flush the fileâs contents.
- Obtain properties and status for a file or directory.
- Obtain the ACL (access control list) of a file or directory.
- Get a list of all the available paths in the Azure Data Lake Storage file system.

For examples of some of these common use cases, see [Examples](azure-data-lake-connector-examples).

## Audience

- New users, read:
  - [Studio Configuration](azure-data-lake-connector-studio)
  - [Examples](azure-data-lake-connector-examples), with use case information for the connector

- Advanced users, read:
  - [XML and Maven Support](azure-data-lake-connector-xml-maven)
  - [Examples](azure-data-lake-connector-examples), with use case information for the connector

## Connection Types

Azure Data Lake Storage Connector connections use the following authentication types:

- Azure Active Directory OAuth 2.0  
  Delegates user authentication to the service hosting the user account
- Shared Access Signature  
  Uses information contained in the SAS token to authorize the client request
- Shared Key Connection  
  Uses a shared access key that is generated by Azure and used for the storage account

## Japanese Character Support

Azure Data Lake Storage Connector supports Japanese characters for path names. If you use Japanese
characters in path names, you must encode the Japanese characters into valid UTF-8 encoded and
escaped URI characters. DataWeave 2.0 provides a function to encode Japanese characters. See
[encodeURI(String)](../../dataweave/latest/dataweave-formats-urlencoded)

File content operation payloads, such as adding content to a file, also support Japanese characters,
and do not require any encoding.

This connector does not support Japanese for Azure Data Lake Storage file system names, as this is
not supported by Azure Data Lake Storage itself.

## Next Step

After you complete the prerequisites, you are ready to create an app and configure the connector
using [Anypoint Studio](azure-data-lake-connector-studio).

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Use Exchange to Discover Connectors, Templates, and Examples](../../connectors/introduction/intro-use-exchange)
- [Azure Data Lake Storage Connector Reference](azure-data-lake-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
